questions.question: Match each IMRaD section to its core purpose.
categories: Give background, engage the reader, and motivate the study
Describe and justify what was done, arguing for replicability
Summarise the findings and refer to tables and figures
Interpret results, answer the RQ, and critically evaluate

correct_mapping.Introduction: Give background, engage the reader, and motivate the study
correct_mapping.Methods: Describe and justify what was done, arguing for replicability
correct_mapping.Results: Summarise the findings and refer to tables and figures
correct_mapping.Discussion: Interpret results, answer the RQ, and critically evaluate

questions.explanation: The Introduction motivates the study, Methods describe and justify for replicability, Results summarise findings with exhibits, and the Discussion interprets and evaluates.

questions.question: Match each CARS move to its description.
categories: Establish the territory: show the topic matters and review what is known
Establish a niche: counterclaim, indicate a gap, or extend prior work
Occupy the niche: state the purpose, RQ, and the paper's structure

correct_mapping.Move 1: Establish the territory: show the topic matters and review what is known
correct_mapping.Move 2: Establish a niche: counterclaim, indicate a gap, or extend prior work
correct_mapping.Move 3: Occupy the niche: state the purpose, RQ, and the paper's structure

questions.explanation: CARS runs Move 1 (establish the territory), Move 2 (establish a niche via counterclaim/gap/extension), and Move 3 (occupy the niche by stating the purpose and RQ).

questions.question: Match each section to its expected voice or tense.
categories: Passive voice and past tense
Past tense for what you found
Present tense when pointing to your tables or figures
As active as possible, with hedging on claims about meaning

correct_mapping.Results findings: Past tense for what you found
correct_mapping.Results exhibits: Present tense when pointing to your tables or figures
correct_mapping.Discussion: As active as possible, with hedging on claims about meaning

questions.explanation: Methods use passive/past, your findings use past, references to your own exhibits use present, and the Discussion is active and hedged.

questions.question: Match each abstract element to its IMRaD part.
categories: Introduction (background)
Introduction (aim)
Method
Results
Discussion
questions.explanation: The abstract's five elements map onto IMRaD: topic and aim from the Introduction, study type/design/materials from Method, main outcome from Results, and conclusion/implications from the Discussion.

questions.question: Match each Methods subsection to the study type whose template it belongs to.
categories: Participant study
Participant study (action steps)
ML study
ML study (results metrics)
questions.explanation: A participant study orders Participants, Materials/Stimuli, Design, Procedure; an ML study orders Dataset, Preprocessing, Implementation, Performance Evaluation.

questions.question: Match each lens used to view a section to what it checks.
categories: Whether the right information is present
How the parts are ordered and connected
Register, voice, and word choice
Tense, punctuation, and number formatting

correct_mapping.Content: Whether the right information is present
correct_mapping.Structure: How the parts are ordered and connected

questions.explanation: Each section is viewed through four lenses in order: Content (what is present), Structure (order and connection), Language (register/voice/word choice), and Grammar and Mechanics (tense, punctuation, numbers).
